notes: feminine singular third person subject; formal 'you' singular
third person plural subject; also formal 'you' plural
li for masc; le for fem plural
to/for you plural; indirect object clitic
to/for them; indirect object clitic (also 'loro' postposed)

indirect mi + direct la → me la
indirect mi + direct li → me li
indirect mi + direct le → me le

indirect ti + direct la → te la
indirect ti + direct li → te li
indirect ti + direct le → te le

indirect le + direct la → gliela (same form as gli+la)
reflexive si + direct lo → se lo
reflexive si + direct la → se la
reflexive si + direct li → se li
reflexive si + direct le → se le
indirect ci + direct lo → ce lo
indirect ci + direct la → ce la
indirect ci + direct li → ce li
indirect ci + direct le → ce le
indirect vi + direct lo → ve lo
indirect vi + direct la → ve la
indirect vi + direct li → ve li
indirect vi + direct le → ve le
indirect gli + direct lo → glielo (same for 3p)
reflexive si + direct lo → se lo
reflexive si + direct li → se li
reflexive si + direct le → se le
partitive/pronoun: of it; of them; some
masculine singular: that (before consonant clusters: z; gn; ps; s+consonant)
masculine singular before most consonants: quel libro
masculine plural before z; gn; ps; s+consonant: quegli studenti
masculine plural before vowel: quegli amici
my — masculine sg/feminine sg/masculine pl/feminine pl
their — invariable; does not agree in gender/number
own — reflexive emphasis; agrees in gender/number
with definite article: il mio libro
with definite article: il tuo libro
with definite article: il suo libro
with definite article: il nostro libro
with definite article: il vostro libro
with definite article: il loro libro (invariable loro)
articles omitted with singular family members: mia madre; tuo fratello
articles kept with plural family: le mie sorelle; i tuoi genitori
article always kept with 'loro': la loro madre
who; whom; which; that — invariable subject or direct object
whom; whose; to/for which — invariable; used with prepositions
who; whoever; those who — invariable; pronoun only; no antecedent
where — invariable; relative adverb of place
